feed.title: AO3 - Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: Kinæsthesia
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 76,513Chapters: 12/?Kudos: 149

Summary: "You are your own worst enemy."- Martha Kent Clark comes home to a city still wearing his symbol like a wound. Bruce comes home to Clark. It takes both of them longer than it should. Features: an opinionated elderly neighbor, a neighborhood that gives a damn, a word in Kryptonian that doesn't translate cleanly, and two men who are catastrophically bad at being afraid in front of each other. The longer version: After the Fortress, after the city's grief and the shaharreth on every wall, Clark Kent comes back to Metropolis and tries to figure out what home means for someone like him. Bruce Wayne is apparently part of that answer. Clark is working on accepting this. This is a story about the specific courage of choosing someone who will die before you do. About the difference between happiness and contentment. About the people who show up for you in your building hallway at six in the morning and the people who put a transponder in your cape because they can't say I was afraid for you any other way. Martha Kent called it. She usually does.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.summary:
Author: Foggyisfoguey

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,457Chapters: 6/6Kudos: 43

entry.title: The Raven Roosts in the Cornflower Blue
entry.summary:
Author: Aurora2Sunset

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 60,659Chapters: 12/30Kudos: 326

Summary: Someone new moved to Smallville, in the house on the other side of the Kent farm. Clark meets the new kid, Bruce Wayne, and his first impression isn't great. He thinks that Bruce is spoiled and rude and out of place. Maybe that's why he blows up at him. But he still saves him when he's in trouble, that's just the kind of person that Clark is. After a while they develop a tentative kind of friendship over the summer. Bruce isn't like anyone else that Clark knows, and he feels connected to him in all of his out of place-ness. His own feelings for the other take him by surprise, he only hopes that he can stomp them down before he ruins their friendship over them. - Smallville AU where Bruce spends the summers there and befriends Clark long before either of them become superheroes.

entry.title: Five weeks, Three days and Fourteen Hours Without You
entry.summary:
Author: Gotham_Blight

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,624Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 42

Summary: Clark's been off on an intergalactic mission for the Lantern Corps. That means he's been away from Bruce for five weeks, three days, fourteen hours, and thirty-three minutes. (Not that he's been counting) He has a lot of time to make up for, and make up for it he does.

entry.title: Wings and Fangs: A Ladyhawke AU
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 14,471Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 42

Summary: Bruce Wayne becomes a hawk at sunrise. Kal-El becomes a wolf at sunset. Ninety seconds of overlap twice a day. Three years of this. They can see each other. Can't touch. Can't speak. Just run toward each other and fail, over and over, while their bodies break and the curse grinds them down to nothing. Ra's al Ghul built it to torture them. Punishment for refusing to serve. For loving each other. For being happy. Tim Drake finds them six months after everyone else gave up looking. He's wounded, obsessed, and the entire hero community thinks he's lost it. He doesn't care. With a guilt-ridden mage, a solar eclipse, and sheer bloody-minded determination, Tim is going to break an unbreakable curse. Even if it kills him. A Ladyhawke AU. Body horror. Transformation curses. Two legends reduced to beasts. And one Robin who refuses to let them suffer alone.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: Boundaries and ways not to cross them
entry.summary:
Author: morallywrong

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,170Chapters: 1/8Kudos: 35

Summary: Bruce had known all day what was coming at him. Like watching a car crash in slow motion, he was unable to do anything about the situation.---Bruce was a man of many faces and he took care of whom he showed which side of himself. When the Justice League gives him the ultimatum to reveal himself or leave the League, he leaves. If only Superman, the man he loved, had shown him support... Instead, the other hero couldn't even look him in the eye.

entry.title: Missing (bat)Man
entry.summary:
Author: LadyRiversoftheNorth

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 16,453Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 49

Summary: This story is a Superbat story inspired by the manhua Missing Love: The Marrying Man. I treat cannon as my own personal playground so there will be some bending where I want to because I am god here. Unlike my other work, this will not have an update schedule, I apologize. Chapters will come as inspiration does, but I do not foresee it being more than a few weeks between each update. TW: Graphic descriptions of torture, violence, and rape in this fiction, (never between the ML and MC!)   When Bruce/Batman gets in over his head and is kidnapped, Superman must step in for Gotham, and for Bruce's ward/sidekick Dick Grayson. But as Clark Kent he must also track down Bruce Wayne...and he is not the world's greatest detective.

entry.title: But you can't take the circus from the boy
entry.summary:
Author: Gotham_Blight

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 19,009Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 250

Summary: Damian Wayne has everything he's ever wanted. A beautiful husband. A precious child. A title of his own. Even his siblings, having experienced horrors beyond their years, are growing as people alongside him. But with everything in his life moving so smoothly, he can't help but wonder when the other shoe will drop. Happiness isn't something that comes easily to Demons, after all.

entry.title: Simplest Words Make The Most Complicated Sentences
entry.summary:
Author: LuKuXing

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 12,527Chapters: 7/?Kudos: 100

Summary: ⎛⎝( ` ᢍ ´ )⎠⎞In a world where your soulmate's first words were written on your body, Bruce couldn't think of anything other than how much he appreciated his soulmate, even if they weren't here. Soulmates were supposed to love you for who you were in your core. Would Bruce's soulmate love him as he is? Would they cherish him? Put up with him? ----- Clark never knew what it meant to have a soulmate. It was distant, something that always reminded him that he wasn't human. Being an outsider was all he was, as the people around him found their soulmates, talked about them like the best thing. Could he even ever feel that, If he doesn't have a soulmate?

entry.summary:
Author: i8thecookie

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 49,937Chapters: 16/18Kudos: 280

Summary: Years ago, in Smallville, Bruce made an impression on Clark. And not a good one. Now Bruce is back in his life—charming, relentless, and completely unaware of the damage he left behind all those years ago. But Clark hasn't forgotten. And while Batman and Superman learn how to trust each other, their civilian identities are busy getting everything wrong.

entry.title: 𝑾𝑶𝑹𝑲𝑷𝑳𝑨𝑪𝑬 𝑽𝑰𝑶𝑳𝑨𝑻𝑰𝑶𝑵 // 𝑨 𝑺𝑼𝑷𝑬𝑹𝑩𝑨𝑻 𝑺𝑻𝑶𝑹𝒀
entry.summary:
Author: battinscnz

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 16,250Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 37

Summary: Clark Kent doesn’t trust billionaires - especially not Bruce Wayne, a man whose charm feels too precise to be anything but calculated. But when he’s assigned to interview him about Wayne Enterprises expanding into Metropolis, keeping his distance quickly becomes impossible, and what should be routine turns into something sharper, charged in ways Clark isn’t prepared for. Somewhere between tension and restraint, every glance lingers too long, every exchange cuts a little deeper, and control starts to slip in quiet, dangerous increments. Clark still doesn’t trust him - and maybe that’s the problem. Because the harder he tries to keep his distance, the more it feels like Bruce Wayne is already too close - and knows it.

entry.title: Conjunction
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 16,519Chapters: 8/?Kudos: 26

Summary: The ring is on his finger. Bruce has been aware of this for forty-seven hours.He keeps looking at it. He is not performing anything about this. He is simply — looking at it. Clark has told Perry, Lois, Martha, and Diana Prince.Bruce has told zero people. This is, he acknowledges, a disparity. — Book Three of the Aphelion trilogy. Bruce doesn't know how not to be a bachelor. Clark is going to teach him. The chair gets moved. The furniture negotiation is ongoing. Clark hums country songs. Clark talks to the monstera plants. Martha has thoughts about the wedding. Alfred has already prepared. The orbit continues. Warmer now. And known.

entry.title: like grey skies (like a bird of paradise)
entry.summary:
Author: rainbow_nerds

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 60,976Chapters: 15/18Kudos: 487

Summary: He sighed to himself and downed the glass of champagne he'd been nursing—non-alcoholic, but enough to convince anyone who might give him a passing glance. He hated this, every time he had to do it. There was one surefire way to derail anyone who began wondering whether maybe Bruce Wayne was doing more than just donating funds to Batman. His tie hung loose around his neck, shirt unbuttoned to halfway down his chest, laughing too easily, pressing close to everyone he spoke to, whispering in ears, playing the part, until he turned and froze on the spot. Clark Kent, in his signature ill-fitting suit, stood holding a notebook and pen, staring at him with barely-concealed glee. Bruce cringed internally. He hated this. He especially hated when it had to be witnessed by someone he actually respected. or: After a Gala, Bruce and Clark end up in bed together. They're friends, just helping each other out, no strings attached. Which would be great, if Bruce hadn't been halfway to head-over-heels since the day they met.
